nhs-surgery-readiness.title: Improving Surgery Readiness at Scale with the NHS
nhs-surgery-readiness.metaDescription: How NHS North East London used Sword Intelligence to make more patients surgery-ready, reduce length of stay, and relieve pressure across surgical pathways.

hero.eyebrow: Case Study: NHS North East London Health and Care Partnership
hero.logoAlt: North East London Health & Care Partnership
hero.imageAlt: Queen’s Hospital exterior at sunrise, North East London

overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: The North East London Health & Care Partnership serves more than 2 million people across 8 local authority areas. Following the COVID-19 pandemic, elective surgery backlogs became a system-wide constraint. Not due to lack of surgical capacity, but because many patients reached the pathway unprepared for surgery, leading to delays, cancellations, and extended hospital stays.

To address this, Sword Intelligence was deployed to identify, engage, and operationally prepare patients ahead of surgery, at scale and without adding clinical headcount. The program focused on improving patients’ physical and mental readiness through structured, ongoing engagement, while continuously generating readiness signals that informed operational decisions.

By shifting preparation upstream and standardizing how readiness was assessed and improved, NE London was able to convert more patients from “unfit” to surgery-ready, reduce length of stay, and relieve pressure across surgical pathways.

panels.quote: “This offered a safe space to share my experience without having to burden my family or make them feel worried. It was amazing for me to have that.”
panels.author: Vanessa

nhs-prehabilitation.title: Cutting Hospital Stays with AI-driven Prehabilitation
nhs-prehabilitation.metaTitle: NHS Black Country Prehabilitation — Sword Intelligence
nhs-prehabilitation.metaDescription: How NHS Black Country ICB used Sword Intelligence to shorten hospital stays, avoid bed days, and save £405k for one NHS Trust through AI-driven surgery prehabilitation.

hero.eyebrow: Case Study: NHS Black Country ICB
hero.logoAlt: NHS Black Country Integrated Care Board
hero.imageAlt: NHS Black Country hospital exterior at dusk

stats.label: saved for one NHS Trust in 3 months

overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: NHS Black Country ICB is one of six Integrated Care Boards across the West Midlands, responsible for the health of 1.26 million people. After the COVID-19 pandemic, the region faced a growing elective surgery backlog — not simply a capacity problem, but a readiness one. Many patients arriving at the point of surgery were not physically or mentally prepared, leading to longer hospital stays, higher complication rates, and greater pressure on clinical teams.

Sword Intelligence was deployed at Dudley Group NHS Foundation Trust as an initial proof of concept, identifying patients on the waiting list and engaging them through a structured agentic surgery prehabilitation program ahead of intermediate-to-major elective procedures. Patients completed a health assessment on enrollment, which shaped their individual program structure. The goal was to shift preparation upstream — improving patients’ physical fitness, nutrition, and activation before they ever reached the theatre.

The program was open to all adult patients awaiting intermediate or major elective surgery at the Trust. 28% enrolled and completed the program. Plans are now in place to extend the offering across the wider Black Country region.

panels.label: Customer
panels.quote: “Collaborating with Sword Intelligence has transformed our preoperative care, leading to markedly better patient outcomes and fewer complications.”
panels.author: Dr Anna Pierson
panels.role:
System Lead for Perioperative Medicine
NHS Black Country ICB

greece.title: Bringing Intelligent Triage to Greece
greece.metaTitle: Intelligent Healthcare for Greece — Sword Intelligence
greece.metaDescription: How the Greek Government and Sword Intelligence built one of Europe’s first national, AI-powered front doors to healthcare — the 1566 National Health Information Line serving more than 10 million citizens.

hero.eyebrow: National Initiative: Hellenic Ministry of Health
hero.imageAlt: Adonis Georgiadis, Minister of Health (Greece), with the Athens skyline behind

overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: The Greek Government, alongside Sword Intelligence, is building one of Europe’s first national, AI-powered front doors to healthcare, serving more than 10 million citizens.
The new National Health Information Line (1566) has quickly become one of the most important public services in the country, guiding citizens and helping them navigate care.

By combining always-on agentic triage with human empathy, the service delivers faster answers, clearer guidance on where to seek care, and consistent regional advice — reducing unnecessary hospital visits while strengthening primary and preventive care.

panels.label: Government
panels.quote: “This partnership will rebuild how care flows through our system, making it faster, clearer, and more centered on people.”
panels.author: Adonis Georgiadis

nhs-surgical-complications.title: Intelligence Helps the NHS Prevent Surgical Complications
nhs-surgical-complications.metaTitle: NHS Cheshire & Merseyside Surgical Complications — Sword Intelligence
nhs-surgical-complications.metaDescription: How NHS Cheshire and Merseyside used Sword Intelligence to cut surgical complications by 72% and post-operative pulmonary complications by 83% with AI-driven surgical readiness.

hero.eyebrow: Case Study: NHS Cheshire and Merseyside ICB
hero.imageAlt: Whiston Hospital exterior at dusk, NHS Cheshire and Merseyside

overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: NHS Cheshire and Merseyside represents one of England’s largest integrated care systems, serving 2.7 million people across 17 NHS Trusts in the North West. Following the COVID-19 pandemic, elective surgery backlogs became a system-wide constraint — not due to lack of surgical capacity, but because a significant proportion of waiting patients carried elevated clinical risk and were arriving at surgery unprepared, driving avoidable complications, extended stays, and readmissions.

To address this, Sword Intelligence deployed a surgical readiness agentic workflow at St Helens and Knowsley Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust as a proof of concept. High-risk patients were identified directly from the waiting list and enrolled in structured, personalized pre-habilitation support before surgery. The approach turned a passive waiting list into an active preparation list.

The program covered a cohort of patients undergoing intermediate-to-major elective surgery across general surgery, orthopaedics, urology, and gynaecology. A matched cohort of eligible patients who chose not to participate served as a control group.

Results demonstrated that pairing individualized risk assessment with targeted pre-habilitation support can meaningfully reduce complications, shorten hospital stays, and cut readmissions — without adding clinical headcount.

panels.label: Customer
panels.quote: “With the evidence so far and the wider benefit to patients, elective recovery and capacity usage, there is a real desire to develop a business case and find funding for long-term expansion.”
panels.author: Prof. Rowan Pritchard-Jones
panels.role:
Medical Director
NHS Cheshire and Merseyside

nhs-deprived-communities.title: AI Surgical Readiness Closes the Gap for Deprived Communities
nhs-deprived-communities.metaTitle: South Tees AI Surgical Readiness — Sword Intelligence
nhs-deprived-communities.metaDescription: How South Tees NHS Foundation Trust used Sword Intelligence to deliver AI-supported pre-habilitation to a digitally underserved population — improving quality of life, shortening stays, and saving £250 per patient.

hero.eyebrow: Case Study: South Tees NHS Foundation Trust
hero.logoAlt: South Tees Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust
hero.imageAlt: James Cook University Hospital exterior, South Tees

stats.label: improvement in patient-reported quality of life

overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: South Tees NHS Foundation Trust serves one of the most socio-economically disadvantaged regions in England, where health inequalities are high. In January 2023, the Trust partnered with Sword Intelligence and Public Health England to launch a hybrid pre-habilitation program for patients awaiting major surgery, offering a choice of digital support or face-to-face care.

The program ran for 11 months, with each patient following an 8-week structured pre-habilitation journey. Patients were recruited from the most deprived postcodes, and the evaluation tracked quality of life, programme preference, length of stay, and cost.
Results showed that nearly half of a high-risk, digitally underserved population chose AI-supported pre-habilitation over in-person care — achieving better outcomes while saving money.

panels.quote: “The feedback from our patients has been overwhelmingly positive since we began working with Sword Intelligence.”

South Tees NHS Foundation Trust
